# Galactic Encyclopedia: Psychohistory

Psychohistory is the mathematical science of predicting the behavior of large populations. Developed by Hari Seldon at Streeling University on Trantor, it combines history, sociology, and statistical mechanics to forecast the broad sweeps of future events.

The discipline rests on two axioms: first, that the population under study must be sufficiently large for statistical treatment to be valid; second, that the population must remain unaware of the predictions, lest their knowledge alter their behavior.

Seldon demonstrated that the Galactic Empire, then at the height of its power, was destined to collapse within five centuries, followed by a period of barbarism lasting thirty thousand years. His Foundation Plan aimed to reduce this interregnum to a single millennium by preserving and extending human knowledge through strategically placed Foundations at the periphery of the Galaxy.

The mathematics of psychohistory remain classified as a Foundation security asset. Only the First and Second Foundations maintain the full equations.
